summary: Stereo Ultra‑Matrix capture of a solid Greek Theater night with soaring jams and a standout Terrapin Station, marred only by occasional crackle.
review: The show is praised for relaxed yet inspired playing – from the opening "Music" through jam‑heavy highlights like "Sugaree," "Rooster," the "China > Rider" sequence and a powerful "Terrapin Station." The sbd‑matrix recording is generally fantastic, offering clear stereo separation, though one reviewer notes a loud crackling glitch during Terrapin. Guest percussionist Baba Olatunji adds extra flavor to the drums segment.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and emphasize strong musical performance with only minor recording glitches, yielding a high combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo Ultra‑Matrix (sbd/aud) mix praised as fantastic and clear; one note of loud crackling during Terrapin Station, otherwise clean and well balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A relaxed first set that builds into a jam‑rich second half; transitions (China > Rider, Around > Saturday) flow smoothly and the encore "Knockin'" caps a satisfying night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is highlighted throughout – meandering soundscapes with Brent, a bright but occasionally tired tone, notable slip‑note tease before Take You Home.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass sits higher in the mix than on adjacent nights, adding presence to the low end.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as aching yet enthusiastic; occasional lyrical humor noted (Nancy/ Ron reference).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent contributed strong jams, chordal cues into Take You Home and memorable interplay with Jerry during spacey sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drum segment is praised as rhythmic and tribal; guest percussionist Baba Olatunji adds a special flavor.
